summary: Iteration 9 — Tested 5 new MHCGED messaging features (auto-share, delete, broadcast, search, read receipts). Backend pytest: 14/17 PASS, 3 FAIL — all 3 failures are real bugs in get_conversation/unread-count which do NOT honor is_deleted nor the ?search= query parameter despite the spec calling for both. Frontend smoke test: all data-testids present and functional; broadcast dialog round-trip works, conv-search works, msg-sent/msg-read indicators render, but thread-search input does NOT filter messages (because backend ignores ?search= for conversation/{peer}).

critical.issue: Does NOT filter is_deleted — soft-deleted messages still appear in the thread. server.py lines 1095-1110 query has no is_deleted filter.

Does NOT accept ?search= query param — spec requires case-insensitive content filter; frontend Messages.jsx line 56 already passes the param but backend ignores it, so thread-search UI input is non-functional.
Does NOT exclude is_deleted messages (server.py line 1115 count_documents only filters by to_user_id + is_read). Deleted messages still inflate unread badge.

minor.issue: Auto-share grants recipient access to attached document regardless of whether sender owns/has access to that doc. As called out in the request other_misc_info, this is intentional UX but is technically a privilege-escalation vector: any logged-in user can pass any existing doc id as attachment_doc_id and force-share it with a third party they choose. Recommend restricting to docs where sender is uploader or already in shared_with.

integration_issues.issue: thread-search input does not filter messages because backend ignores ?search= on /api/messages/conversation/{peer_id}. UI displays full thread even when typing a non-matching query.

tests_executed.details: PASS: auto-share single, auto-share broadcast, sender-delete-own, non-sender-403, admin-delete-any, last_message excludes deleted, all 5 broadcast tests, 2 conv-search tests, read receipt flow. FAIL: deleted_msg_not_in_conversation, unread_count_excludes_deleted, thread_search_filter.

All test-ids present: conv-search, broadcast-btn, broadcast-content, broadcast-attach, broadcast-submit, broadcast-check-{id} (×2), thread-search, msg-sent-{id}, msg-read-{id}, msg-delete-{id}. Broadcast dialog opens, recipients listed, content typed, submitted successfully (verified message appeared as last_message in conv list). conv-search filter works. msg-sent/msg-read icons render correctly. thread-search input ACCEPTS input but does NOT filter (backend bug).

action_items: Fix get_conversation (server.py ~line 1097): add 'is_deleted': {'$ne': True} to the find() filter so soft-deleted messages don't appear in the thread.
Fix get_conversation: accept search: Optional[str] = Query(None) and AND a {'content': {'$regex': re.escape(search), '$options': 'i'}} clause when present, mirroring list_conversations.
Fix messages_unread_count (server.py line 1115): add 'is_deleted': {'$ne': True} to count_documents filter.
(Optional security hardening) Restrict auto-share to attachments the sender already owns or has access to.
critical_code_review_comments: get_conversation and unread-count were not updated to handle is_deleted soft-delete flag, but list_conversations was — inconsistent. Same field, three endpoints, only one is correct.

Frontend Messages.jsx already passes ?search= to /messages/conversation/{peerId} (line 56-57 loadThread), so the contract is silently broken: UI sends a parameter the backend silently drops. Either implement server-side filter OR document the inconsistency and switch to client-side filter for thread.

Auto-share auto-grants shared_with to recipient without checking sender's ownership/access — a logged-in user can probe arbitrary doc ids via attachment_doc_id and grant a third-party access. Mitigated only by attachment id being random uuid, but still flagged.

msg-read-{id} (CheckCheck) icon is rendered with className 'text-emerald-300' on a dark green message bubble bg — low contrast against the dark message bubble background. Consider a lighter/white check icon for better visibility on the sender's own (dark) bubbles.

success_rate.backend: 82% (14/17 tests pass — 3 failures are real backend bugs, NOT test issues)
success_rate.frontend: 95% (all test-ids present + interactions work, except thread-search filter which is blocked by backend bug)

test_credentials: admin@mhcged.cg / Admin@2026 (verified). Created/destroyed transient agent users with prefix TEST_uitest_msg_*.
seed_data_creation: Created 2 agent users + several test docs during pytest, all cleaned up in fixture teardown. One real broadcast message 'TEST UI broadcast hello' was sent from admin to DSIC during frontend smoke (not cleaned).
context_for_next_testing_agent: The 3 backend failures are all the same class of bug (missing is_deleted filter and missing search param on the per-thread endpoint and unread-count). Fix is ~5 lines in /app/backend/server.py lines 1095-1116. After fix, rerun: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_messaging_new.py -v. All other features (auto-share, delete authz, broadcast validation, conv-search, read receipts) are GREEN. Frontend already passes ?search= and uses correct test-ids — no frontend changes needed for the search fix, just backend.
rca_of_the_issue: Spec called for is_deleted and ?search= to be added to THREE endpoints: list_conversations, get_conversation, unread-count. The developer applied both changes to list_conversations only. get_conversation (server.py lines 1095-1110) still has a naive {$or:[from/to]} filter with neither is_deleted nor search. unread-count (line 1115) still has a naive {to_user_id, is_read:false} filter. Repro for is_deleted leak: POST /messages then DELETE /messages/{id} then GET /messages/conversation/{peer} → deleted message still in messages[]. Repro for search: GET /messages/conversation/{peer}?search=foo → returns all messages, not filtered. Repro for unread: send→delete→count → still incremented.
